chunks.title: Course Information / Catalog Description / Special Course Designation / Additional Course Information
chunks.content:
Meeting Times: Meeting Type: LEC 
Meeting Days: MWF 
Start Time: 3:00PM 
End Time: 3:50PM 
Start Date: 01/12/2026 
End Date: 05/05/2026 
Meeting Location: HRBB 113
Credit Hours: 3

Database Systems. (3-0). Credit 3. Database modeling techniques; expressiveness
in query languages including knowledge representation; manipulation languages
data models; physical data organization; relational database design theory; query
processing; transaction management and recovery; distributed data management.

None

All course information will be posted in canvas. You should check your course
announcements, course material and due dates daily. It is strongly recommended that you
keep up with the course material and do not fall behind. Start working on assignments as
soon as they are assigned.
This course is using Canvas Learning Management System. Students submit their
homework and projects via Canvas.

chunks.title: Prerequisite: / Course Prerequisites
chunks.content:
Prerequisite: CSCE 310 or approval of Instructor.

Prerequisite/Corequisite(s): CSCE 310 or approval of Instructor.

chunks.title: Course Learning Outcomes
chunks.content:
Upon completion of this course, the learner will be able to:
understand the fundamentals of database design 
develop database systems for applications
understand relational database theory
master efficient query processing techniques
deal with system issues related to database systems

chunks.content:

chunks.title: Grading Policy / Grading Scale:
chunks.content:
The course has 3 homework assignments, 2 course projects, a midterm exam,
and a final exam.
Exams are taken in-class. 
Homework: 6*3 = 18%, Projects: 22+15 = 37%, Midterm Exam: 15%, Final Exam:
30%.

Grading Scale: A = 90-100%, B = 70-89%, C = 60-69%, F = 0-59%.

chunks.title: Late Work Policy / Course Specific Late Work Policy / Course Specific Attendance Policy / Makeup Work Policy
chunks.content:
Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not
considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy (Student Rule 7).

The homework and project assignments are due on the designated due dates at the
beginning of class. No late submissions will be accepted. Discuss unusual
circumstances in advance with the instructor.

Class attendance is strongly recommended. The student is responsible for any
material missed. Missed exams may be made up only when the absence is
university sanctioned. Medical reasons or other extenuating conditions beyond the
control of the student must be properly documented. Discuss unusual
circumstances in advance with the instructor when possible.

Students will be excused from attending class on the day of a graded activity or
when attendance contributes to a student’s grade, for the reasons stated in
Student Rule 7, or other reason deemed appropriate by the instructor.
Please refer to Student Rule 7 in its entirety for information about makeup work,
including definitions, and related documentation and timelines.
Absences related to Title IX of the Education Amendments of 1972 may necessitate
a period of more than 30 days for make-up work, and the timeframe for make-up
work should be agreed upon by the student and instructor” (Student Rule 7,
Section 7.4.1).
“The instructor is under no obligation to provide an opportunity for the student to
make up work missed because of an unexcused absence” (Student Rule 7, Section
7.4.2).
Students who request an excused absence are expected to uphold the Aggie Honor
Code and Student Conduct Code. (See Student Rule 24).

chunks.title: Course Schedule
chunks.content:
Week 1 (1/12- ): Introduction to database systems 
Week 2 (1/19- ): Database design
Week 3 (1/26- ): Functional dependency and relation normalization
Week 4 (2/2- ): SQL
Week 5 (2/9- ): SQL (continued), homework #1 due
Week 6 (2/16- ): Constraints and triggers
Week 7 (2/23- ): Memory hierarchy 
Week 8 (3/2- ): Query optimization, homework #2 due, midterm exam (3/6)
Week 9 (3/9- ): Spring Break. No classes
Week 10 (3/16- ): Query optimization (continued), course project #1 due
Week 11 (3/23- ): Index structures
Week 12 (3/30- ): System crash recovery
Week 13 (4/6- ): System crash recovery (continued), homework #3 due 
Week 14 (4/13- ): Concurrency control
Week 15 (4/20- ): Transaction processing 
Week 16 (4/27): Bigdata, course summary, course project #2 due.
May 4, Monday, 10:30am–12:30pm: Final exam

chunks.title: AI Statement
chunks.content: The use of AI is strictly prohibited in this course.

chunks.title: Course Summary
chunks.content:
CSCE 608 | Database Systems | Spring 2026
Instructor: Jianer Chen | j-chen3@tamu.edu
Meets: MWF 3:00PM - 3:50PM | HRBB 113
Topics: Functional dependency, relation normalization, Index structures, Memory hierarchy, Concurrency control, Transaction processing, System crash recovery, Query optimization, Distributed data management, knowledge representation, manipulation languages, data models, physical data organization, relational database design theory, efficient query processing techniques, system issues related to database systems, database modeling techniques, database design fundamentals, database systems development, SQL, Constraints, triggers, Bigdata, database systems
Tools: Canvas Learning Management System
Prerequisites: CSCE 310 or approval of Instructor.
